description: Phase 1 of Learning Curation: soft-archive infrastructure (retired_at column), curate retire/unretire commands, and retired_at IS NULL filters across all 14 retrieval queries.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a retired learning (manually set retired_at) and verifies it's excluded from FTS5 search
Test verifies retired learning excluded from LIKE fallback search
Test verifies retired learning excluded from unfiltered recency query
Test verifies retired learning excluded from pattern-matching retrieval
Test verifies retired learning excluded from UCB fallback (both variants)
Test verifies retired learning excluded from bandit total_window_shows aggregate
Test verifies retired learning excluded from ingestion dedup check (learning_exists)
Test verifies retired learning excluded from learnings list and count
Test verifies retired learning excluded from export load_learnings query
Test verifies retired learning excluded from export calculate_statistics outcome counts
Test verifies retired learning excluded from stats query_learning_counts
Test verifies retired learning excluded from import dedup check
Test verifies get_learning() by ID STILL returns retired learning (exempt)
Test verifies apply_learning still works for retired learning by ID (exempt)
Known-bad discriminator: test that a naive implementation without filtering would include retired learnings
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] or expected to fail until FEAT-002)
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation that verify retired learnings (retired_at IS NOT NULL) are excluded from every retrieval, list, count, and aggregate query. Must cover all 14 query locations. Also test that get_learning() by ID still returns retired learnings (exempt).
edgeCases: Retired learning with retired_at set appears in FTS5 search results — must be excluded
Retired learning counted in SELECT COUNT(*) FROM learnings — must be excluded from count
Retired learning counted in SUM(window_shown) aggregate — must be excluded
get_learning() by ID returns retired learning — must NOT be excluded (admin view)
learning_exists() dedup check: retired learning with same outcome+title should not block new creation — debatable but current PRD says YES filter

failureModes.cause: Missing retired_at IS NULL filter in one of 14 queries
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test for that specific query path fails, identifying the missed location

failureModes.cause: Filter added to get_learning() by ID (should be exempt)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test verifying retired learning accessible by ID fails

invariants: A learning with retired_at IS NOT NULL must never appear in any recall/retrieval result
A learning with retired_at IS NOT NULL must still be accessible by direct ID lookup
Active learning count must equal total minus retired count
Retiring a learning must not affect other active learnings
userStories.notes: This is the most critical test in Phase 1. Every one of the 14 YES-filter queries must be verified. Group tests by module (retrieval, recall, bandit, commands). Tests will fail until FEAT-001 (migration) and FEAT-002 (filters) are implemented.
userStories.title: Initial tests for retired_at filtering across all retrieval paths

acceptanceCriteria: Test: learning matching criterion 1 (age >= 90 days, confidence=low, times_applied=0) is a candidate
Test: learning matching criterion 2 (times_shown >= 10, times_applied=0) is a candidate
Test: learning matching criterion 3 (times_shown >= 20, application rate < 0.05) is a candidate
Test: learning NOT matching any criterion is NOT a candidate
Test: dry_run=true identifies candidates but does NOT set retired_at
Test: dry_run=false sets retired_at on all candidates
Test: custom thresholds (min_age_days, min_shows, max_rate) change candidate set
Test: 0 candidates returns empty result, no errors
Known-bad discriminator: high-confidence learning with 0 applications should NOT be retired by criterion 1 (requires low confidence)
userStories.description: Write tests for the retirement candidate identification logic: three criteria (old+low+unapplied, shown-but-never-applied, low-application-rate), dry-run mode, and actual retirement execution. Tests will fail until FEAT-004 is implemented.
edgeCases: 0 learnings in database — return empty result, no error
Learning exactly at age threshold boundary (89 days vs 90 days) — 89 should not be candidate
Learning with times_shown=9 (just under min_shows=10) — should not be candidate for criterion 2
Learning with application rate exactly at 0.05 threshold — should NOT be candidate (< 0.05, not <=)
Already-retired learning — should not appear as candidate again

failureModes.cause: Integer division in application rate (times_applied/times_shown returns 0 for 1/20)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Cast to REAL before division to get 0.05 not 0

failureModes.cause: SQLite julianday comparison off-by-one at date boundaries
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Use >= for age comparison, test with boundary values

invariants: dry_run=true must never modify the database
Candidate count in result must match actual retired_at updates when dry_run=false
Each candidate must have a human-readable reason string
userStories.notes: Use in-memory SQLite with schema+migrations applied. Set created_at manually for age-based tests. Tests depend on FEAT-001 for the retired_at column to exist.
userStories.title: Initial tests for curate retire candidate identification

acceptanceCriteria: Test: unretire sets retired_at = NULL on a retired learning
Test: unretired learning reappears in recall/list queries
Test: unretire on non-existent ID returns appropriate error
Test: unretire on already-active learning (retired_at IS NULL) returns appropriate error/no-op
Test: unretire multiple IDs in one call
Known-bad discriminator: unretiring an already-active learning should not succeed silently (must distinguish from actual unretire)
userStories.description: Write tests for the unretire subcommand: restoring retired learnings to active status, error handling for invalid/non-retired IDs.
edgeCases: Unretire non-existent learning ID — return NotFound error
Unretire learning that is already active (retired_at IS NULL) — return error or no-op with message
Unretire multiple IDs where some are valid and some are not — handle partial success

failureModes.cause: Unretire succeeds silently on non-existent ID
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Return NotFound error with learning ID in message

invariants: After unretire, learning.retired_at must be NULL
After unretire, learning must appear in standard recall queries
Unretire must not modify any field other than retired_at
userStories.notes: Simpler than retire — straightforward CRUD operation. Tests depend on FEAT-001 for schema.
userStories.title: Initial tests for curate unretire

acceptanceCriteria: src/db/migrations/v8.rs exists with up_sql and down_sql
up_sql: ALTER TABLE learnings ADD COLUMN retired_at TEXT DEFAULT NULL
down_sql: comment explaining SQLite limitation (can't drop columns pre-3.35)
v8 registered in MIGRATIONS array in migrations/mod.rs
CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION bumped to 8
cargo test passes (existing migration tests still work)
userStories.description: Create migration v8.rs that adds `retired_at TEXT DEFAULT NULL` to the learnings table. Register in migrations/mod.rs, bump CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION to 8.
userStories.notes: Follow exact pattern from v7.rs. The down_sql can be a no-op like v7 since SQLite doesn't support DROP COLUMN in older versions. NULL columns are safe to leave.

correctness: Migration must be idempotent when run on already-migrated DB (handled by version check)
Column must default to NULL so existing learnings remain active
style: Follow exact pattern from v7.rs: static MIGRATION, version/description/up_sql/down_sql

userStories.title: Migration v8: add retired_at column to learnings table

acceptanceCriteria: fts5.rs: FTS5 search query (line ~148) adds AND l.retired_at IS NULL
fts5.rs: LIKE fallback query (line ~202) adds AND retired_at IS NULL
fts5.rs: Unfiltered recency query (line ~257) adds WHERE retired_at IS NULL
patterns.rs: load_learnings_with_applicability (line ~215) adds AND retired_at IS NULL
recall/mod.rs: load_ucb_fallback all-learnings (line ~183) adds WHERE retired_at IS NULL
recall/mod.rs: load_ucb_fallback with exclusion (line ~202) adds AND retired_at IS NULL
bandit.rs: get_total_window_shows (line ~240) adds WHERE retired_at IS NULL
ingestion/mod.rs: learning_exists (line ~133) adds AND retired_at IS NULL
commands/learnings.rs: COUNT(*) (line ~66) adds WHERE retired_at IS NULL
commands/learnings.rs: list queries (lines ~78, ~91) add WHERE retired_at IS NULL
export/progress.rs: load for export (line ~195) adds WHERE retired_at IS NULL
export/progress.rs: count by outcome (line ~367) adds WHERE retired_at IS NULL
import_learnings/mod.rs: load_existing_keys (line ~138) adds WHERE retired_at IS NULL
stats.rs: query_learning_counts (line ~147) adds WHERE retired_at IS NULL
All TEST-INIT-001 tests pass
userStories.description: Add `AND retired_at IS NULL` (or `WHERE retired_at IS NULL`) to all 14 queries that select from the learnings table for retrieval, listing, counting, or aggregation. Exempt queries: get_learning() by ID, apply_learning by ID, get_window_stats by ID, delete, bandit refresh_sliding_window.
userStories.notes: This is mechanical but error-prone (14 locations). Work through each file systematically. The PRD's #1 risk is missing a filter in one location. The test from TEST-INIT-001 will catch any missed locations.

correctness: ALL 14 queries must have the filter — missing even one means retired learnings leak into recall
get_learning() by ID must NOT have the filter — needed for admin/unretire operations
For JOIN queries (FTS5), filter on l.retired_at IS NULL (the learnings alias)
performance: No index needed on retired_at for now — most learnings will be active (NULL), so full scan is fine for the minority of retired rows
style: Use consistent placement: AND retired_at IS NULL at end of WHERE clause
For queries using append_common_filters(), consider adding the filter there if all callers need it

userStories.title: Add retired_at IS NULL filter to all 14 retrieval queries

acceptanceCriteria: CurateAction enum in cli/commands.rs with Retire, Unretire, and placeholder variants
Retire variant has: --dry-run, --min-age-days (default 90), --min-shows (default 10), --max-rate (default 0.05)
Unretire variant has: learning_ids (Vec<i64>, required positional args)
Commands::Curate variant with #[command(subcommand)] action: CurateAction
src/commands/curate/mod.rs created with module declarations
src/commands/curate/types.rs created with RetireResult, UnretireResult, RetirementCandidate structs
src/commands/curate/output.rs created with format_retire_text, format_unretire_text
src/commands/mod.rs exports curate module
task-mgr curate --help shows subcommands
userStories.description: Create the CLI command structure for `curate` with subcommands. Add CurateAction enum in commands.rs, Curate variant in Commands enum, create src/commands/curate/ module with types.rs, mod.rs, output.rs stubs.
userStories.notes: Follow the existing pattern from other subcommand enums (if any exist, e.g. run begin/end). CurateAction is similar to how other subcommand groups are organized. Result types must derive Serialize for JSON output mode.

correctness: All result types must derive Serialize for JSON output
Default values for CLI flags must match PRD: 90 days, 10 shows, 0.05 rate
style: Follow existing CLI patterns: clap derive, #[arg(long)] with defaults, #[command(after_help)] for examples
RetirementCandidate must include reason string for human-readable dry-run output

userStories.title: CLI scaffolding: CurateAction enum, Curate variant, module structure

acceptanceCriteria: curate_retire() in src/commands/curate/retire.rs accepts conn, dry_run, min_age_days, min_shows, max_rate
SQL query identifies candidates matching ANY of 3 criteria with correct threshold comparisons
Criterion 1: age >= min_age_days AND confidence = 'low' AND times_applied = 0
Criterion 2: times_shown >= min_shows AND times_applied = 0
Criterion 3: times_shown >= min_shows*2 AND CAST(times_applied AS REAL)/CAST(times_shown AS REAL) < max_rate
Each candidate has a human-readable reason string explaining which criterion matched
dry_run=true returns candidates without DB modification
dry_run=false sets retired_at=datetime('now') in a single transaction
Returns RetireResult with dry_run flag, candidates_found, learnings_retired, candidates list
Already-retired learnings excluded from candidate query (WHERE retired_at IS NULL)
All TEST-INIT-002 tests pass
userStories.description: Implement the curate_retire() function that identifies stale learnings based on three criteria and either previews (dry-run) or soft-archives them by setting retired_at.
userStories.notes: Use CAST(times_applied AS REAL) / CAST(times_shown AS REAL) to avoid integer division. Use julianday('now') - julianday(created_at) for age calculation. Single SQL query with OR for all criteria, then post-process to assign reason strings.

correctness: Integer division trap: MUST cast to REAL for application rate calculation
Age comparison: julianday arithmetic must use >= not > for the threshold
Already-retired learnings must be excluded from candidates (WHERE retired_at IS NULL)
performance: Single SQL query for candidate identification, not N+1
Single UPDATE statement for batch retirement (not one per candidate)
style: Follow existing command pattern: Params struct, Result struct, main function
Reason strings should be human-readable: 'Low confidence, never applied, 95 days old'

userStories.title: Implement curate retire: candidate identification and soft-archive

acceptanceCriteria: curate_unretire() in src/commands/curate/unretire.rs accepts conn and learning_ids (Vec<i64>)
Validates each ID exists in database
Validates each ID is currently retired (retired_at IS NOT NULL)
Sets retired_at = NULL for valid IDs
Returns UnretireResult with count of restored learnings and any errors
Uses a transaction for atomicity
All TEST-INIT-003 tests pass
userStories.description: Implement curate_unretire() that takes learning IDs and sets retired_at = NULL, restoring them to active status.
userStories.notes: Simple CRUD operation. Check existence with get_learning(), then UPDATE. For invalid IDs (not found or not retired), collect errors but continue processing remaining IDs.

correctness: Must validate learning exists AND is retired before unretiring
Must not silently succeed on non-existent or already-active IDs
style: Follow error handling pattern: collect per-ID results, return aggregate

userStories.title: Implement curate unretire: restore soft-archived learnings

LockGuard acquired before DB operations

impl_text_formattable!(UnretireResult, format_unretire_text) in handlers.rs

correctness: LockGuard must be acquired before any DB write operation
style: Follow exact dispatch pattern from existing commands in main.rs

acceptanceCriteria: LearningsListResult gains a `total_including_retired` field (or similar)
List command queries both: active count (WHERE retired_at IS NULL) and total count (no filter)
Text output shows 'Showing N of M active learnings (R retired)' when retired > 0

JSON output includes the new field
userStories.description: Modify the learnings list command to show both active count and total count (including retired). The existing count/list queries already filter by retired_at IS NULL (from FEAT-002), so add a separate total-including-retired count.
userStories.notes: Small enhancement to the existing list command. Add one more COUNT query without the retired_at filter. Only change the output format when retired count > 0 to avoid noise.

correctness: total_including_retired must count ALL learnings (no retired_at filter)
active count must use retired_at IS NULL filter
style: Only show retired count in output when > 0 to avoid noise for users without retired learnings

userStories.title: Update learnings list to show active vs total count
